title: Selection and collocation split candidate
notes: Ninth-round mutation generated from the preposition category/selection pressure. It separates payload preposition choice, collocational rigidity, and argument-linking selection so fixed prepositions are not automatically operator values. It is a scoped module for preposition and complement-selection boundary cases, not a general account.

conditioning_axes.community: Specify the speech or judgment community before interpreting selection, licensing, repair, or attestation.
conditioning_axes.genre: Specify genre or constructional context before interpreting PP choice, collocation, or repair.
conditioning_axes.medium: Specify whether evidence comes from speech, edited prose, elicited written examples, corpus text, or another medium.
conditioning_axes.task_framing: Specify whether the task asks for paraphrase availability, acceptability, grammaticality, category assignment, or repair.

edges.rationale: Argument-linking selection can be part of the operator-like configuration for head-selected or grammaticized preposition patterns.
A complement or argument-linking function is warrant that a preposition choice belongs to a selected constructional repertoire.
Analogous constructions can support an argument-linking analysis, but the force depends on constructional context.
For scoped selection cases, a compatible operator value is required for licensing but does not by itself settle licensing.
Open payload preposition choices can affect acceptability through content, fit, or construal without becoming licensing failures.
Fixed lexical co-occurrence can affect acceptability or naturalness without itself being operator value.
Collocational fixedness can be contextual warrant for preemption when competitors occupy the same lexical slot, but it is not automatically argument-linking selection.
Available competitors are part of what makes absence or rarity count as preemption rather than mere low opportunity.
Opportunity mass is part of what makes a missing or rare selected form probative.
Observed frequency is the numerator for opportunity-normalized attestation.
Opportunity mass is the denominator for interpreting attestation.
Opportunity-normalized attestation is warrant for licensing in the specified community and constructional context.
Strong preemption weakens warrant for licensing in the specified opportunity and competitor context.
Lower community licensing predicts greater repair or reformulation pressure in the selected constructional context.
Repair pressure is contextual evidence for licensing trouble, not the target itself.
Community licensing is contextual warrant for grammaticality attribution.
Acceptability reports provide contextual evidence for attribution without defining grammatical status.
Genre conditions whether a PP choice, selected complement, or repair is appropriate in context.
Medium affects how preposition choices, examples, and repairs are interpreted.
Higher register or genre fit tends to increase reported acceptability in situated judgment tasks.
Task framing can foreground paraphrase availability, category assignment, naturalness, or grammaticality.
